text: Nature (Computational Science, December 2025) published a comprehensive survey of environmental acoustic intelligence through Sound Event Localization and Detection (SELD) — the joint task of identifying what sounds are active (siren, dog bark, speech) and where they originate in 3D space — establishing SELD as the foundation for audio-based AI that gives machines "hearing" capabilities analogous to computer vision for sight.

Springer AI Review (March 2025) surveyed deep learning for environmental sound recognition on embedded devices — reviewing architectures (CNN, CRNN, Transformer) optimized for edge deployment — demonstrating that TinyML audio models (<100KB) achieve 85-92% accuracy on standard benchmarks (ESC-50, UrbanSound8K) while operating at 10mW on microcontrollers, enabling always-on environmental monitoring.
